title: practical english usage - Part 218
content:

As soon as she arrived. ►    293.1 x Dear Ms  Williams, How  are you getting on? ►    289 Note 13 x x   Dear Mr  James Carter, ... ►    289 Note 6 x   We   planned wonderful plans. ►    287.1 x  The  banks have raised interest paid monthly rates. ►    285.1 story. ►    283.3 x   He confessed to 114  murders; the police did not believe, however, his it was decided to close three of the smaller branches. ►    283.1 x   The  results were disappointing for the third year running, consequently x  The  wall, it needed painting. ►    282.3 x  Lucy told Alice she had made a bad mistake. ►    282.2 x   Dad ’ s just cut Dad  shaving. ►    282 Do  you   know     what's    wrong     with    these,    and   why?

grammar •  281   formality INFORMAL: Who did they elect?

Whom  did they elect?

It was her that first saw what to do.

It was she who first saw what to do.

formal and informal language (►   174). Compare: Subject and object forms of pronouns (e.g. I and me) are used differently in INFORMAL: Neither of us like him.

FORMAL:         Neither of us likes him.

language, and by plural forms in informal language (►   156.2, 157.2). Compare: Some determiners are followed more often by singular verb forms in formal INFORMAL: The man she married...

The man whom she married.. .

Some relative structures are different (►  234). Compare: INFORMAL: Which century did he live in?

In which century did he live?

Prepositions come at the end of certain structures in informal language (►  209).

It's gone. It isn't possible.

It has gone. It is not possible.

informal speech and writing. Compare: For example, contracted auxiliary verbs and negatives (►   337)  are common in Some grammatical structures have different formal and informal versions.

English speakers do not have to know two ways of saying everything.

Most words and expressions are neither formal nor informal, but neutral - some of his customers to understand) station, put up perhaps because the manager felt this would be easier for The toilets are outside round the back. (handwritten notice in the same petrol Oxfordshire petrol station) Customer toilets are at the rear of the building. (printed notice in an makes them preferable.

informal, but informal writing and formal speech are used when the situation emails to one’ s family. Writing is more often formal, and speech is more often mostly used in informal situations: for example in conversations with friends, or or polite conversations with strangers. And some words and structures are example in official notices, business letters or reports, meetings or conferences, situations, when people are careful about how  they express themselves: for However, there are some words and structures which  are mostly used in formal older or more important people. English does not have a system of this kind.

languages, for example, there are very complicated rules about how  to speak to Most people speak and write in different ways on different occasions. In some 1  formal     and   informal       language 281  formality                           __ grammar •  282  pronouns and other proforms readers the day before.

Here is a note from the Independent newspaper apologising for confusing Lucy told Alice she had made a bad mistake. (Who  made the mistake?) reference is clear.

When  pronouns such as he, she, it are used, it is important to make sure the 2 avoiding       ambiguity For more details, ►    275-280 (ellipsis) and ►    585 (so).

The only options were to dance or to go for a walk: they went for a walk.

She was offered potatoes or rice, and decided on rice.

However, repetition is necessary and normal when alternatives are discussed.

(More normal than .. . to stay at the Royal Hotel.) The Royal Hotel was highly recommended, so the party agreed to stay there.

(More normal than .. . they believed that the road was open.) They were not sure whether the road was open, but they believed so.
